steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the best description of the text's overall structure. Map the three sentences: (1) defines community science; (2) lists three benefits — conservation, youth interest, more data; (3) names a specific study by Merrill that used this approach.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It introduces the topic of a scientific study, describes the study's importance, and then presents the study's results.’: The text never presents Merrill's results — it only mentions the study exists. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It argues for a new approach to scientific research, comments on the public's opinion about the approach, and then describes how that approach was applied in a certain study.’: The text doesn't argue for a new approach or comment on public opinion. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It identifies a particular approach to research, lists some benefits of that approach, and then mentions a study in which that approach was used.’: Sentence 1 identifies the approach, sentence 2 lists benefits, sentence 3 mentions a study using it. Exact match. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It describes the development of a type of scientific collaboration, shows how that type of collaboration has been used in a particular field of study, and then suggests future collaborative projects.’: The text describes no historical development and suggests no future projects. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
